sourceMovie.title: Wicked
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Wicked (2024) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Beastly
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wicked clicked for you, Beastly (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Cinderella
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wicked clicked for you, Cinderella (1950) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Enchanted
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wicked clicked for you, Enchanted (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wicked clicked for you, The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wicked?
faq.answer: Try Beastly, Cinderella, Enchanted—each shares DNA with Wicked while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wicked?
faq.answer: Wicked (2024) is best described as Drama, Romance, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wicked part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Wicked Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
